question: Extend the `Animal` hierarchy (from Q14) to use virtual functions for the `sound()` method. Create a `Zoo` class that takes a `Animal*` and calls its `sound()` method, showcasing polymorphic behavior.

test_cases.expected:
Zoo is open for business!
Adding Max to the zoo.
Adding Luna to the zoo.
Adding Charlie to the zoo.

--- All animals in the zoo are making sounds! ---
Max barks: Woof! Woof!
Luna meows: Meow!
Charlie barks: Woof! Woof!
--- End of animal sounds ---

Program ending. Unique pointers will clean up automatically.
Zoo is closing. All animals will be automatically destroyed.
Animal Charlie destroyed.
Charlie (Dog) destroyed.
Animal Luna destroyed.
Luna (Cat) destroyed.
Animal Max destroyed.
Max (Dog) destroyed.
